description: If the buffer's DPI is below this, upsample to it.
If the buffer's DPI is above this, downsample to it.
Search range in degrees (±). Coarse search runs in 1° steps then refines.
Apply rotation only when detected skew exceeds this (degrees).
Fine-search angle step in degrees.
If false, only records meta.skew without rotating.
0 = white border. >1 = k-means cluster count for background colour detection.
Padding around each detected text bbox before cropping.
Max child crops per page (1 = single page, 2 = two-page spread, 0 = no cap).
Minimum DPI scale-factor delta to trigger a resize.
ROI margin (≤ margin_mm) propagated downstream for Binarizer masking.
Downsample to this DPI for detection; full-res used for cropping. Empty = no downscale.
Column-merge score cutoff for combining horizontally-overlapping boxes.
Weight of the inter-box gap term in the merge score.
Weight of the width-similarity term in the merge score.
Cap on the normalized inter-box gap used in the merge score.
Text detector backend. auto = apple_vision on macOS else east → dbnet → heuristic.

Reject layouts whose (p95−p5) pixel range is below this fraction of the max across all merged layouts in the scan. Polarity-agnostic, robust to sparse text. Legitimate text > 0.7; bleed-through < 0.5. 0 = disabled.
Binarization algorithm (doxapy: wolf++/wolf/sauvola/niblack/nick/… plus otsu). gray keeps grayscale; none passes through.
Wolf-family window in mm. Each doxa family has its own window_mm_<family> / window_px_<family> / k_<family> (e.g. window_mm_sauvola, k_sauvola).
Wolf-family threshold bias. Higher = whiter. Per-family k_<family> exists for each algorithm.
If meta.roi is set, erode the mask N iterations before applying.
Morphological close (0–10) after thresholding.
Where to source text-line bboxes from.
Below this many detected lines, fall back to passthrough.
Horizontal MORPH_CLOSE kernel for line bridging (mm at page DPI). Fallback when <30 char-like CCs available.
Adaptive MORPH_CLOSE kernel width = mult × median char height. Bridges words into full-line spans.
TEXT_MAX_THICKNESS = mult × median char height (≈ ascender + x-height + descender). Drops multi-line bridged blobs.
EDGE_MAX_LENGTH = mult × median char height. Max Euclidean gap between adjacent cinfos the span-builder will link. 3 ≈ one wide justified word gap + margin.
RANSAC trials for the column-edge fits (per-side) and the horizontal VP fit.
RANSAC inlier tolerance for the VP fit (degrees).
Extra padding around the rectified column quad.
Remap interpolation mode. auto = nearest on BW inputs, cubic otherwise.
Reject if column-quad covers less of the crop than this.
Skip Zhang-He metric upgrade when quad is nearly axis-aligned.
Reject ZH aspect if it drifts more than this fraction from bbox aspect.
Reject if median baseline tilt exceeds this.
Sanity cap on recovered w/h.
Save VP/line overlay as debug image to meta.
Surface the 'Normalize character width' checkbox in the GUI (pre-ticked).
Optimizer backend. auto = MLX → padded JAX → Powell. mlx forces Apple Metal (MLX) on top of padded JAX. jax skips MLX, uses padded JAX L-BFGS-B. powell uses SciPy Powell (no JAX/MLX).

Reject the dewarp if remap goes more than this many pixels out of bounds. Bumped to 400 because the page outline (footer + page number area) routinely extrapolates 200-300 px below the last detected span — benign white fringe, not a real failure.
Minimum span count to run the cubic fit. Below this, the optimizer is under-constrained and passthrough is safer.

Drop spans whose width is less than this fraction of the widest span in the scan. Cuts page-number / author-line / footer fragments that bias the cubic fit toward false curvature; keep ≥ the footnote block so the fit's support reaches the page bottom (else footnotes get extrapolated/fanned). 0 = disabled.
Mask margin in mm — defines the page-extent rectangle for span detection.
Pre-dewarp white padding around the crop (mm).
Decimation factor for the remap grid. Lower = sharper but slower.
Penalty on rvec[0]² (camera tilt). Does NOT regularize cubic α/β; use cubic_cost for that.

L2 penalty on cubic slopes α, β. 0 = page-dewarp default (no regularizer). Previously needed at 1.0 to mask a span-linking bug that produced phantom curl on flat pages; with that bug fixed (see EDGE_MAX_LENGTH = 3·h_med, span_mask isolation) the regularizer biases real curl out — leave at 0. Crank it up only if a real input over-curls.
Normalized camera focal length. Overridden by calibration if present.
Horizontal MORPH_CLOSE kernel fallback (mm at analysis DPI). Used when <30 char-like CCs available.
Adaptive MORPH_CLOSE kernel width = mult × median char height. Bridges words into full-line spans.
TEXT_MAX_THICKNESS = mult × median char height (≈ ascender + x-height + descender). Drops multi-line bridged blobs.
EDGE_MAX_LENGTH = mult × median char height. Max Euclidean gap between adjacent cinfos the span-builder will link. 3 ≈ one wide justified word gap + margin.
Downsample to this DPI for span analysis; full-res for the remap.
Save intermediate visualisations as debug_artifacts.
Surface the 'Normalize character width' checkbox in the GUI (pre-ticked).
CSS-style margin in mm. "10" all sides; "10 20" V H; "10 20 30 40" L R T B.
Same as margin_mm but in pixels (overrides margin_mm when set).
